generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Prioritize repairs that address unresolved identifiers or invalid type declarations within AGREE contracts.
Ensure AGREE contracts use only visible AADL features and declared helper variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `int`, `real`, and `bool` for AGREE scalar type declarations.
Guarantees should relate visible outputs to visible inputs.
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not present in the AADL model.
Do not place implementation-level bindings (e.g., subcomponent paths) within component type annexes.
Do not attempt to fix AGREE errors by modifying the AADL architecture.
